label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this. The question is asking why the author said a loud "thank you" to the doorman. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to recall the story. The author's family was at a restaurant, and there was a man with a dirty face who opened the door for them. The kids commented on his smell, and the mom explained to look beyond the dirt. Then when she went back to get the car-sick pills, the doorman was opening the door again. She said a loud thank you. Then she ended up giving him a meal through the waitress, and the kids were part of that. The key part here is why she said thank you. The options are about teaching gratitude, showing love, helping him, or just being polite. The author's reflection at the end says that she wanted to teach her children by example, showing that they can choose to show gratitude. So the act of saying thank you was part of that lesson. The loud thank you was not just a polite gesture but a deliberate action to model gratitude in front of the kids, leading to the subsequent action of giving him a meal. Option A says the purpose was to give the children a lesson in gratitude. That aligns with the author's reflection. Option D is about polite manner, but the story indicates it was more than just politeness; it was about setting an example. The other
